food_name: Soto Betawi (Jakarta spiced beef and offal soup in coconut milk)
food_subgroup: Spiced beef and offal soup in rich coconut milk broth

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has no specific concept for Soto Betawi. Closest parent is coconut milk-based spiced beef soup (FOODON:03315595 — beef soup, as closest parent). No exact FoodOn URI available; null retained pending FoodOn expansion.
ontology.foodex2_description: Spiced beef and offal soup in coconut milk broth (Indonesian soto betawi style)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: A00MN is the closest FoodEx2 parent for composite meat-based soups. No exact Soto Betawi code exists in FoodEx2 as of 2026.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept exists for Soto Betawi or Indonesian coconut beef soup as of 2026.

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving of Soto Betawi is one bowl of soup only, approximately 400 g, as commonly served at Indonesian restaurants and Jakarta food stalls.

nutrient_source.title: Soto Betawi — FAO/INFOODS Southeast Asia Regional Food Composition Table
nutrient_source.organization: FAO/INFOODS Southeast Asia Regional Food Composition Table
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced from FAO/INFOODS SEA regional food composition table entry for Soto Betawi. Where micronutrient detail was not available in the SEA table, values were cross-referenced against USDA FoodData Central entries for beef offal and coconut milk components (USDA SR Legacy). Monounsaturated and polyunsaturated fat breakdown not reported in the SEA table; set to -1.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; analogous estimate based on low-carbohydrate coconut milk beef soup
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Soto Betawi. GI estimated at 30 (low) based on analogous low-carbohydrate coconut milk-based meat soups in the University of Sydney GI Database. Carbohydrate content is 4.5 g/100 g; at 400 g serving = 18 g available carbohydrate. GL = (30 × 18) / 100 = 5.4 per serving. The dominant macronutrients are fat and protein; the low carbohydrate load strongly supports a low GI classification.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content — 398 mg per 100 g; a 400 g bowl delivers approximately 1,592 mg sodium, representing 69–80% of the WHO recommended daily limit of 2,000 mg.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit Soto Betawi to occasional consumption and request reduced-salt preparation; the high sodium load from seasoned broth makes regular intake clinically inadvisable for blood pressure management.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate content (4.5 g/100 g) is favourable for glycaemic control, but very high saturated fat (7.5 g/100 g) from full-fat coconut milk may impair insulin sensitivity with frequent consumption.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Soto Betawi has a low glycaemic index and low carbohydrate load, making it acceptable for blood glucose management in people with Type 2 Diabetes, but the high saturated fat content warrants portion control and infrequent consumption to avoid worsening insulin resistance.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Very high saturated fat (7.5 g/100 g) and high cholesterol (125 mg/100 g) from full-fat coconut milk and beef offal; a 400 g serving delivers 30 g saturated fat (150% of the 20 g/day recommended limit) and 500 mg dietary cholesterol.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease or dyslipidaemia should avoid regular consumption of Soto Betawi due to its exceptionally high saturated fat and cholesterol content, which are established risk factors for atherosclerosis and adverse cardiovascular events.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High saturated fat and arachidonic acid from beef offal may promote systemic inflammation.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should limit Soto Betawi due to its high saturated fat content and the pro-inflammatory arachidonic acid contributed by offal components such as lung and tripe.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Soto Betawi is a rich, opaque white-yellow soup originating from the Betawi people of Jakarta, Indonesia. Unlike the clear-broth Soto Ayam of Malay tradition, Soto Betawi uses full-fat coconut milk to create a milky, creamy, unctuous broth that is intensely flavoured and visually distinctive. The soup contains beef and traditionally includes beef offal such as lungs, tripe, or brain, simmered with aromatic spices including lemongrass, galangal, bay leaf, and kaffir lime leaf. It is served with sliced tomato, fried shallots, and emping crackers. Soto Betawi is one of the most calorie-dense soups in Indonesian cuisine, with 148 kcal per 100 g and a high fat content of 10.8 g per 100 g, of which 7.5 g is saturated fat from the coconut milk base. A standard 400 g bowl delivers approximately 592 kcal, 42 g fat, 30 g saturated fat, 500 mg cholesterol, and 1,592 mg sodium. The glycaemic index is low (estimated ~30) due to the minimal carbohydrate content of 4.5 g per 100 g. Clinically, the dish is high concern for cardiovascular disease and hypertension due to its saturated fat, cholesterol, and sodium load. It is halal, gluten-free, and dairy-free. It is commonly found at Indonesian restaurants, Jakarta food stalls, and Indonesian food courts in Singapore.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced from FAO/INFOODS SEA regional food composition table for Soto Betawi, which is an approved primary source for Southeast Asian dishes. Confidence is medium rather than high because the FAO/INFOODS SEA entry represents a composite dish with variable preparation; monounsaturated and polyunsaturated fat breakdown are not reported. GI is an analogous estimate, not a direct measurement.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation data for Soto Betawi not available in the FAO/INFOODS SEA table or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported in the FAO/INFOODS SEA source for this composite dish. trans_fat_g of 0.2 g/100 g is reported from the source and reflects minor naturally occurring trans fats from ruminant-derived beef and offal. GI is an analogous estimate (~30, low) based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for low-carbohydrate coconut milk meat soups; no direct GI measurement for Soto Betawi exists in the published literature as of 2026. Glycaemic load per 400 g serving = 5.4 (low). The dish is classified as halal, gluten-free, and dairy-free based on traditional preparation without soy sauce or dairy additives; individual restaurant preparations may vary.
